moduleFinalProject.completion.p1: That's all nine modules, from how a blockchain works to how to keep yourself safe using one.
moduleFinalProject.completion.p2: Modules stay here if you ever want to revisit one, and the glossary terms across the course are worth bookmarking as a quick reference.
moduleFinalProject.completion.title: You've Completed Nodra Academy
moduleFinalProject.hero.description: Not a new topic, a guided review of everything the Academy covered, closing with one comprehensive quiz.
moduleFinalProject.hero.stat1.label: Difficulty
moduleFinalProject.hero.stat2.label: Duration
moduleFinalProject.hero.stat3.label: Format
moduleFinalProject.hero.stat4.label: Sections
moduleFinalProject.hero.title: Final Project
moduleFinalProject.introduction.p1: The Final Project isn't a new topic. It's a guided walk back through everything Modules 01 through 09 covered, foundations, networks, assets, DeFi, and security, tying the pieces together before the course-wide quiz.
moduleFinalProject.introduction.p2: Each recap section below is intentionally short, a reminder, not a rewrite. If a summary doesn't fully click, that module's link takes you straight back to it.
moduleFinalProject.introduction.title: Introduction
moduleFinalProject.meta.title: Final Project — Nodra Academy
moduleFinalProject.puttingTogether.p1: Say you're evaluating a new protocol promising a stablecoin paying an unusually high yield. Every module in this course gives you one piece of that evaluation.
moduleFinalProject.puttingTogether.p2: Which network is it deployed on, and what does that imply about its security assumptions (Module 03)? What actually backs the stablecoin, and is that disclosed (Module 08)? Where does the advertised yield come from, and is it sustainable (Module 07)? Has the contract been audited, and what does that audit actually cover (Module 09)?
moduleFinalProject.puttingTogether.p3: None of these questions require exotic knowledge, they're the same handful of questions from this course, applied together. That's the actual skill this Academy was built to teach.
moduleFinalProject.puttingTogether.title: Putting It Together
moduleFinalProject.quiz.intro: This quiz draws from every module in the Academy. Incorrect answers link back to the module that covers it, in case a refresher helps.
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q1: 1. What makes a blockchain a distributed ledger rather than a normal database?
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q1.feedbackIncor: Not quite, review Module 01 — Blockchain and try again.
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q10: 10. When evaluating a new protocol, which combination of questions from this course matters most?
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q10.feedbackInco: Not quite, review the Academy overview and try again.
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q10o1: A. Only how large its community is
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q10o2: B. What network it's on, what backs it, where its yield comes from, and what its audit actually covers
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q10o3: C. Only its token price history
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q10o4: D. Only who the anonymous founders are
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q1o1: A. It only stores financial data
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q1o2: B. It's copied and synchronized across many independent nodes instead of one server
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q1o3: C. It can be edited by anyone at any time
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q1o4: D. It requires a company to operate it
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q2: 2. What does a crypto wallet actually hold?
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q2.feedbackIncor: Not quite, review Module 02 — Wallets and try again.
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q2o1: A. Your actual coins and tokens
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q2o2: B. The keys that prove ownership of assets that live on the network
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q2o3: C. A copy of the entire blockchain
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q2o4: D. Nothing, it's just a login for an exchange
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q3: 3. What is a Layer 2 built to do?
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q3.feedbackIncor: Not quite, review Module 03 — Networks and try again.
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q3o1: A. Replace Layer 1 security entirely with its own
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q3o2: B. Scale on top of a Layer 1's existing security
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q3o4: D. Function only as a stablecoin issuer
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q4: 4. What's the key difference between a coin and a token?
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q4.feedbackIncor: Not quite, review Module 04 — Assets and try again.
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q4o1: A. A coin is native to its own network, a token is issued by a smart contract on top of one
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q4o2: B. Tokens are always worth more than coins
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q4o3: C. Coins can only be used for gas fees
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q4o4: D. There is no real difference
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q5: 5. In an automated market maker, what replaces a traditional order book?
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q5.feedbackIncor: Not quite, review Module 05 — DeFi and try again.
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q5o1: A. A central company setting prices manually
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q5o2: B. A liquidity pool priced by a pricing formula
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q5o4: D. A single large market maker
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q6: 6. What does a lending protocol's health factor track?
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q6.feedbackIncor: Not quite, review Module 06 — Lending and try again.
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q6o1: A. How much interest a lender has earned so far
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q6o2: B. How close a borrowing position is to being liquidated
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q6o3: C. The total number of users on the protocol
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q6o4: D. The protocol's trading volume that day
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q7: 7. Before trusting an advertised yield, what should you ask first?
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q7.feedbackIncor: Not quite, review Module 07 — Yield and try again.
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q7o1: A. How many followers the project has on social media
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q7o2: B. Where the yield actually comes from, and whether it will still exist next month
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q7o3: C. What color the project's logo is
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q7o4: D. How long the token's name is
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q8: 8. What is the single most useful question before trusting a stablecoin's peg?
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q8.feedbackIncor: Not quite, review Module 08 — Stablecoins and try again.
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q8o1: A. How many exchanges list it
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q8o3: C. How long ago it launched
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q8o4: D. Whether its name sounds trustworthy
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q9: 9. What is the single largest cause of loss in crypto?
moduleFinalProject.quiz.q9.feedbackIncor: Not quite, review Module 09 — Security and try again.
moduleFinalProject.quiz.title: Final Quiz
moduleFinalProject.recapDefi.p1: Module 05 introduced DeFi's building blocks, decentralized exchanges priced by automated market makers instead of an order book, and the impermanent loss that liquidity providers take on in exchange for fees.
moduleFinalProject.recapDefi.p2: Module 06 covered lending protocols, replacing a bank's credit check with collateral and a health factor that determines when a position gets liquidated.
moduleFinalProject.recapDefi.p3: Module 07 stepped back to ask where an advertised yield actually comes from, staking rewards, trading fees, or token emissions, and whether it's still there next month.
moduleFinalProject.recapDefi.p4: Module 08 covered the three ways a stablecoin tries to hold its peg, fiat reserves, crypto over-collateralization, or algorithmic incentives, and why each fails differently when it fails.
moduleFinalProject.recapDefi.title: Recap: DeFi Track
moduleFinalProject.recapFoundations.p1: Module 01 covered the blockchain itself: a distributed ledger replicated across independent nodes that agree on a shared history through consensus, instead of trusting a single server.
moduleFinalProject.recapFoundations.p2: Module 02 covered wallets: they don't hold your assets, they hold the keys that prove ownership of assets that already live on the network, which is why key management is the whole game.
moduleFinalProject.recapNetworksAssets.p: Module 03 covered how different networks trade off decentralization, security and speed differently, and how Layer 2s and bridges connect them, each with their own risk.
Module 04 covered the difference between a coin (native to its network) and a token (issued by a smart contract on top of one), and how to actually verify what you're holding.
moduleFinalProject.recapSecurity.p1: Module 09 covered where losses actually come from, phishing far more than exploits, what an audit does and doesn't guarantee, and the habits that remove most realistic risk: separate wallets by purpose, never share a seed phrase, read what you sign.
moduleFinalProject.recapSecurity.title: Recap: Security
moduleFinalProject.summary.p1: Nine modules, one underlying habit: ask what actually backs something, where a number actually comes from, and who actually controls it, before trusting a network, an asset, a yield, or a peg.
moduleFinalProject.summary.p2: The final quiz below draws one or two questions from every module in the course. It's meant as a genuine check of the whole journey, not just this recap page.
moduleFinalProject.summary.title: Summary
moduleFinalProject.toc.title: Contents
